story_clusters.summary: Crude oil prices remain near $100 as mixed signals emerge regarding a potential Iran war peace deal. The UAE's ADNOC has resumed liquefied natural gas (LNG) shipments to India via the Strait of Hormuz, with one tanker reportedly en route. Additionally, Iran has released 10 Indian sailors detained near Jask Port since 2025.

perspectives.unique_angle: SIR valid, but ECI needs to help deleted voters, says Supreme Court in big ruling The Supreme Court said on Wednesday that the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise of ECI has a direct nexus with

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the Election Commission of India's (ECI) power to conduct the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls, stating it advances free and fair elections. The Court also asked the ECI to forward to the Centre the names of persons deleted from the 2003 Bihar electoral roll due to doubtful citizenship. A judgment on the legality of the ECI's SIR is expected on May 27.

This will be Vijay's first official trip to New Delhi after assuming office as the state's CM.

story_clusters.summary: Assam’s state assembly is considering a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill aimed at regulating personal laws, including polygamy and live-in relationships. The proposed legislation has been framed as a measure to protect women’s rights, with Assam’s Chief Minister stating it would ensure security for Muslim women, while NDA members emphasized it is not directed against any religion. The bill includes provisions on inheritance and live-in partnerships, according to multiple reports.

story_clusters.summary: India and Canada are aiming to finalize a free trade agreement by the end of November, with discussions accelerated after a meeting between Canadian Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland and India’s Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal. Freeland described the potential deal as a "game changer," while Goyal stated it could be completed a month ahead of schedule.

story_clusters.summary: As of April 2026, India's total telecom subscriber base reached 1,216.18 million, with a reported tele-density of 87.11%, according to TRAI's official release. The data reflects a slight decline in urban tele-density to 132.31% and a marginal increase in rural tele-density to 62.18%, as highlighted by PIB.

articles.title: Party Estopped From Challenging Award On The Ground Of Mandate Expiry After Accepting Extension Of Arbitrator's Mandate: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a party cannot later challenge an arbitration award on the grounds that the arbitrator's mandate had expired if they participated in the proceedings without objecting to the mandate's extension. The dispute involved the Gujarat Water Supply and Sewerage Board and Saryu Plastics Pvt. Ltd. over supply contracts. The ruling clarifies procedural fairness in arbitration, impacting how parties engage in such proceedings. The bench of Justices Pamidighantam Sri Narasimha an
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday (May 26) has held that a party who continues to participate in arbitration proceedings without objecting to the extension of the arbitrator's mandate cannot later challenge the award on the ground that the mandate had expired. A bench of Justice Pamidighantam Sri Narasimha and Justice Alok Aradhe heard the dispute that arose out of arbitration proceedings between the Gujarat Water Supply and Sewerage Board and Saryu Plastics Pvt. Ltd. relating to supply contracts for

articles.title: NGT Faces Criticism for Judicial Overreach Through Expanding Suo Moto Powers
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) is facing criticism for expanding its suo moto powers, which allow it to take up cases without formal petitions. Concerns include inconsistent expert reports, ad hoc committees, and relaxed evidence standards in NGT proceedings. Critics warn that weak scientific scrutiny could undermine the credibility of environmental adjudication in India. The article highlights potential risks to environmental policy and governance due to these judicial practices.
articles.raw_summary: The article discusses concerns over inconsistent expert reports, ad hoc committees, and relaxed evidentiary standards in NGT proceedings. It warns that weak scientific scrutiny may compromise the credibility of environmental adjudication. The post NGT Faces Criticism for Judicial Overreach Through Expanding Suo Moto Powers appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: New E-Way Bill Rules 2026: Mandatory Ship-To GSTIN & E-Way Bill Closure Facility
articles.summary: The GST advisory introduces new rules for E-Way Bills starting in 2026, requiring mandatory reporting of the 'Ship To' GSTIN for Bill To – Ship To transactions. Unregistered consignees must be reported using 'URP'. A new facility to close E-Way Bills has also been introduced. These changes aim to streamline tax compliance and reporting for goods transportation under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The latest GST advisory requires mandatory reporting of Ship To GSTIN while generating E-Way Bills for Bill To – Ship To transactions. Unregistered consignees must be reported using “URP”. The post New E-Way Bill Rules 2026: Mandatory Ship-To GSTIN   E-Way Bill Closure Facility appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: DVO Estimate Alone Can’t Create ‘Unexplained Investment’ – ITAT Deletes Additions on Hostel Construction
articles.summary: The Bangalore ITAT held that mere differences between declared construction cost and DVO estimates cannot sustain additions under Section 69B without independent evidence of unaccounted investment. The Tribunal deleted additions relating to hostel construction expenditure. articles.title: WB government directs districts to set up holding centres for detained illegal foreigners
articles.summary: The West Bengal government has instructed all districts to establish holding centers for detained illegal foreigners. This follows Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's announcement of a new 'detect, delete and deport' policy for illegal infiltrators. Earlier, the Union Ministry of Home Affairs had directed states to identify and verify illegal immigrants from Bangladesh and Myanmar within 30 days. The move raises concerns about detention and deportation practices.
articles.raw_summary: The development comes days after Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that his government would follow a new policy of “detect, delete and deport” while dealing with “illegal infiltrators”. Last year in May, Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) directed all States and Union Territories to identify and verify illegal immigrants from Bangladesh and Myanmar (Rohingyas) within 30 days

articles.title: AP HC Quashes GST Revision Order as It Was Passed Beyond Three-Year Limitation
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh High Court struck down a GST revision order issued by tax authorities, ruling it was time-barred beyond the three-year limitation period. The court rejected the authorities' argument that COVID-19-related Supreme Court extensions justified the delay. This decision underscores strict adherence to statutory timelines in tax proceedings and may influence future GST enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Court ruled that tax authorities could not rely on COVID-related extension of limitation granted by the Supreme Court to justify delayed revision proceedings. The impugned GST revision order was therefore declared time-barred. The post AP HC Quashes GST Revision Order as It Was Passed Beyond Three-Year Limitation appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: From polygamy and live-in relationships to inheritance: What Assam’s sweeping UCC Bill seeks to regulate
articles.summary: Assam's state government has introduced a sweeping Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill to regulate personal laws, including polygamy, live-in relationships, and inheritance. The bill aims to standardize civil laws across religious communities. This legislative action follows the state's push for uniformity in personal laws, which has significant implications for civil liberties and governance. The bill is currently in the legislative process and seeks to address longstanding debates over personal law

articles.title: BREAKING| Supreme Court Upholds Election Commission's Power To Conduct SIR Of Electoral Rolls, Says It Advances Free & Fair Elections
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Election Commission of India (ECI) has the authority to conduct the Special Summary Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. The Court held that the ECI's procedure did not violate the law and aligns with the constitutional objective of ensuring free and fair elections. This decision reinforces the ECI's regulatory role in maintaining accurate voter rolls.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the procedure adopted by the ECI in conducting SIR did not violate the law and had nexus with the Constitutional goal of free and fair elections.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside directions from the Himachal Pradesh High Court to the state government, ruling that policy decisions such as fixing Minimum Support Price (MSP) and framing schemes fall under the executive and legislature's domain, not the judiciary. The top court emphasized that courts cannot overstep into policy-making or executive functions. This decision underscores the constitutional separation of powers and limits judicial intervention in policy matters.

articles.title: The judiciary’s role in complete justice
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India can use Article 142 to pass orders ensuring complete justice when existing laws fall short. This power, while acting as a constitutional safeguard, has drawn criticism for potentially overstepping the roles of the Executive or Legislature. The discussion highlights the judiciary's authority and its impact on governance.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court is empowered under Article 142 to pass necessary orders to deliver complete justice where existing laws fail to provide a specific remedy. While this power acts as a ‘constitutional safety valve’, critics argue that it may lead to judicial encroachment upon the domain of the Executive or the Legislature

articles.title: Assessment Void if Section 143(2) Notice Issued by Wrong Assessing Officer
articles.summary: A tax scrutiny assessment is invalid if the mandatory notice under Section 143(2) of the Income Tax Act is issued by an officer without proper jurisdiction. The article highlights that such jurisdictional defects cannot be corrected later by transferring the case. This ruling impacts tax administration and ensures adherence to legal procedures in tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The article explains that a scrutiny assessment becomes invalid when the mandatory notice under Section 143(2) is issued by an officer lacking lawful pecuniary jurisdiction. It highlights that such jurisdictional defects cannot be cured through later transfer of the case. articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN) will require the Ship-To GSTIN to be mandatory in E-Way Bills for Bill-To/Ship-To transactions starting 15 June 2026. articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to hold off on interest rate hikes. Inflation risks are rising due to fuel prices and monsoon uncertainty. This cautious approach aims to balance economic growth and price stability.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a life imprisonment sentence can be modified to a fixed-term sentence if the convict has already served over 14 years. articles.title: PSU closure: Govt sets in motion new move after years wasted in stop-start process
articles.summary: The Indian government is pushing ministries to speed up the closure of unprofitable state-owned companies. Progress has been slow since a policy was approved eight years ago, with only one company successfully shut down. The move aims to address delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes, which have raised concerns over inefficiency in public sector management.

Only one company has effectively ceased operations. The government aims to expedite these closures, addressing concerns over delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes.

articles.title: Shadow fleet pollution is burdening under-resourced nations
articles.summary: A fleet of aging, poorly regulated fuel tankers—often used to bypass sanctions—is causing significant environmental pollution. These ships, operating outside standard regulations, are primarily impacting poorer countries with limited resources to address the damage. The article highlights the broader issue of how global maritime loopholes contribute to environmental harm in vulnerable nations.

articles.title: Government operationalises amended insolvency law, sets 14-day admission deadline for NCLT
articles.summary: On May 26, the Indian government implemented amendments to the insolvency law to speed up bankruptcy resolution for companies. The new rules prioritize lenders in cash distribution and set a 14-day deadline for the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) to admit insolvency cases. This aims to streamline financial recovery processes for creditors.
articles.raw_summary: On May 26, significant updates to the insolvency law took effect, geared towards hastening the resolution of companies facing bankruptcy. The new regulations not only bring forth novel ideas but also refine existing processes. Lenders will now have precedence when it comes to cash distribution, streamlining the financial recovery.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that written statements filed beyond the 120-day mandatory period under the amended Code of Civil Procedure (CPC) cannot be accepted in commercial suits. This decision enforces strict compliance with timelines introduced by the Commercial Courts Act. The ruling impacts how commercial cases are processed in Indian courts.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that written statements filed after expiry of the mandatory 120-day period under the amended CPC cannot be taken on record in commercial suits. articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that deleting a person's name from the National Register of Indian Citizens (NRC) does not automatically revoke their citizenship. This ruling clarifies the legal status of individuals affected by such deletions.

articles.title: Uploading GST Orders on Portal Alone Does Not Automatically Start Limitation Period: Allahabad HC

This decision impacts how tax authorities assess income based on Form 26AS, ensuring fairness and accuracy in tax assessments.

articles.title: Global Market: BOJ’s Ueda warns oil shocks can trigger lasting inflation if wages and expectations rise
articles.summary: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda warned that oil price shocks could lead to lasting inflation if they influence wages and inflation expectations. He emphasized that the impact depends on broader economic factors like wage growth and inflation trends, drawing lessons from Japan's past experiences. The remarks highlight the need for central banks to consider systemic economic responses beyond short-term energy price movements.
articles.raw_summary: Central banks must consider broader economic factors beyond isolated oil price movements, as temporary energy shocks can fuel persistent inflation if they influence wages and pricing behavior. Governor Ueda highlighted that the impact of oil price surges depends on prevailing inflation expectations and wage growth trends, with lessons drawn from Japan's past oil shocks.

This issue highlights potential governance gaps in the tribunal's dress code policy.

Ideally, the dress code for Judicial and Technical Members should be distinct. However, at the NCLT, the dress code for Judicial and Technical Members is identical, showing no difference. articles.title: Exempt income expenses must be strictly excluded from book profits to compute MAT
articles.summary: The article explains that tax authorities must strictly exclude expenses related to exempt income when calculating book profits for Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) under Section 115JB. It clarifies that exempt income expenses cannot be automatically added back without verifying actual expenditures recorded in the books of accounts that have a direct connection to tax-free earnings. This interpretation impacts how companies compute MAT, a key tax mechanism in India.
articles.raw_summary: Eempt income, which were disallowed under Section 14A could not be automatically added back to compute the book profit for Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) under Section 115JB without pinpointing real, actual expenditures recorded in the books of accounts by tax authorities that possess a direct nexus with the tax-free earnings. The post Exempt income expenses must be strictly excluded from book profits to compute MAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Appellate Court Reversing Acquittal Must Itself Hear Convict On Sentence, Cannot Remand To Trial Court For Sentence Alone : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that if an appellate court overturns an acquittal and finds the accused guilty, it must itself impose the sentence rather than sending the matter back to the trial court. The Court cited Section 386(a) of the Criminal Procedure Code (Cr.P.C.), stating that the appellate court is required to pass a sentence according to law. This decision clarifies procedural obligations for higher courts in criminal appeals.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday (May 26) held that if an Appellate Court, while hearing an appeal against an acquittal, �finds the accused guilty, then it cannot relegate the matter to the trial court for imposing the sentence. The Appellate Court itself has to hear the convict on sentence. “It can be seen from Section 386(a), Cr.P.C., that where in an appeal from an order of acquittal, the court hearing the appeal finds the accused guilty it is required to pass a sentence on him according to law.”

The decision clarifies the scope of tax provisions related to deemed dividends.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Addition Due to Absence of Evidence Supporting Excel Sheet Entry
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Chandigarh deleted a tax addition made by the Assessing Officer after finding that the addition was based solely on an unverified Excel sheet entry without supporting evidence. The Tribunal ruled that suspicion alone cannot justify such additions under Section 69 of the Income Tax Act. This decision underscores the need for proper documentation and corroborative proof in tax assessments.

This ruling impacts the nation's import-export status.

articles.title: Only Profit Margin on Unrecorded Sales Taxable Under Section 69A: ITAT Nagpur
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Nagpur ruled that only the gross profit margin from unrecorded sales can be taxed under Section 69A of the Income Tax Act, not the entire sales amount. The Revenue Department's appeal seeking to tax the full sales amount was dismissed. This decision clarifies the scope of taxable income for unrecorded transactions and impacts how tax authorities assess such cases.

It emphasizes human dignity and the common good as guiding principles for AI governance.

articles.title: Abandoned Wind Project Expenses Revenue in Nature Due to No Enduring Asset: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that expenses incurred on an abandoned wind power project were revenue in nature since no enduring asset was created. The case was sent back to the Assessing Officer (AO) for verification of supporting evidence. The ruling impacts how such expenses are treated for tax purposes in India's renewable energy sector.

articles.title: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, halt transit charges under proposed deal with US: Report
articles.summary: The US and Iran are discussing a proposed deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz—one of the world's most vital oil shipping lanes—30 days after an agreement to end hostilities in the Middle East. This development is significant for India due to its heavy reliance on oil imports from the Gulf, potentially impacting energy prices and supply chains.

articles.title: Tata Electronics plans to start chip packaging at upcoming Assam unit
articles.summary: Tata Electronics, part of the Tata Group, is expanding its chip packaging operations by setting up a high-volume manufacturing unit in Jagiroad, Assam. The move follows small-scale shipments from its first facility in Karnataka and aims to meet growing demand in automotive and industrial sectors. The expansion is expected to enhance the company's global credibility and scalability in semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.raw_summary: While the Tata Group company has already initiated small-scale shipments from its first commercial OSAT facility in Vemagal, Karnataka, the strategic pivot to the greenfield high-volume manufacturing unit in Jagiroad, Assam is aimed at building trust among global clients about the company’s capabilities while ensuring immediate scalability to meet surging automotive and industrial demand.

The lawsuit alleges that Instagram is designed to be addictive for young users. This is part of a growing trend of legal challenges against social media companies over child and teen safety concerns.

This decision follows major drug busts by the Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB), including the seizure of 227 kg of Captagon and the extradition of trafficker Salim Dola. The move highlights the government's focus on combating drug trafficking and strengthening enforcement agencies.

The incident raises questions about free speech, online harassment, and the legal boundaries of political discourse in India.

articles.title: What's involved in talks to end the US-Iran war?
articles.summary: The US and Iran are negotiating a framework to end hostilities, including a US naval blockade in exchange for Iran ensuring safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz. The talks aim to stabilize the region and prevent disruptions to global oil shipments, which could affect India's energy imports and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: The framework is focused on an end to the war and a US naval blockade, in exchange for Tehran taking steps to ensure safe transit in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.summary: Chinese tech giant Huawei announced a new method for making semiconductors that could help it avoid US restrictions on advanced chipmaking equipment. The company has faced US sanctions over espionage concerns, which it denies. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and geopolitical tensions, including implications for India's technology and trade policies.

articles.title: Bellatrix readying ultra-low orbit satellites
articles.summary: Bellatrix, a Bengaluru-based company, is developing ultra-low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellites with targeted launches in 2027 or 2028. This project aims to address challenges posed by extreme atmospheric drag in very low orbits, an area currently underexplored globally. The initiative could position India as a leader in advanced satellite operations, potentially influencing future space policy and regulatory frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: The Bengaluru-based company is targeting launches in 2027 or 2028 and is positioning the project as a potential breakthrough in very low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellite operations, an area that remains largely unexplored globally because of the extreme atmospheric drag at those altitudes.

articles.title: I4C warns of phishing scam targeting iPhone users via fake Apple support messages
articles.summary: The Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C) has warned iPhone users about a phishing scam where scammers impersonate Apple Support to steal Apple IDs and OTPs via malicious links. Victims risk losing access to their devices and accounts. The scam underscores the need for vigilance and the use of Apple's official 'Find Devices' service for recovery.
articles.raw_summary: Beware of a cunning phishing scam targeting iPhone users who have lost their devices. Scammers, posing as Apple Support, are sending fake messages with malicious links to steal your Apple ID and OTP. This allows them to access your account and remove your device. Always verify links and use Apple's official 'Find Devices' service for recovery.

Despite these overruns, expenditure on these projects indicates steady implementation progress.

articles.summary: India is now Sri Lanka's second-largest export market, overtaking the United Kingdom. This shift occurred between January and April of this year. Coconut products and IT services performed well.

articles.title: Ex-JPMorgan Banker Chirayu Rana Left Without a Lawyer Hours Before Court Hearing
articles.summary: Former JPMorgan banker Chirayu Rana has accused a senior executive, Lorna Hajdini, of drugging him, subjecting him to racial abuse, and coercing him into non-consensual sexual acts. Rana was left without legal representation just hours before a court hearing. The case highlights issues of workplace misconduct and potential gaps in corporate governance or legal protections for employees in India.
articles.raw_summary: Chirayu Rana has accused JPMorgan executive director Lorna Hajdini of drugging him, racial abuse and coercing him into non-consensual sexual acts.

articles.title: How are degraded coral reefs restored in India? [Explainer]
articles.summary: Coral reefs, often called the rainforests of the sea, are among the most productive and vibrant ecosystems on earth.
